manifest.name: Rust Test Intent & Coverage Map
manifest.description: Catalogs the intent and scope of the Rust test suite across 99 rust files. This manifest tracks test_role, tested_behavior, risk_covered, and target_components to enable agents to quickly locate relevant tests, understand coverage gaps, and identify regression risks.

analyzers.name: rust-test-coverage-intent::file-content::default
analyzers.description: Analyzes Rust files to extract test coverage intent, identifying test roles, frameworks, behaviors, and risks with strict validation.

fields.name: file_extension
fields.description: The file extension without the leading dot (e.g., `js`, `md`), or `null` if none.

fields.name: has_test
fields.description: `true` if this file contains executable tests, test macros, assertions, fuzz targets, benchmark validation, or test harness logic. `false` for pure fixtures even if used by tests, and for non‑test files.

fields.name: test_frameworks
fields.description: Rust test mechanisms or project conventions used, e.g., `["cargo-test", "cfg-test", "rust-test-attr", "rgtest-macro", "proptest", "quickcheck", "compiletest", "cargo-fuzz", "criterion", "custom-harness"]`. **Always an array**, never a string.

fields.name: tested_behavior
fields.description: 3‑10 behavior‑level lowercase kebab-case labels. Prefer action‑object names: `"detect-binary-file"`, `"handle-nul-byte"`, etc. **Always an array**; empty if not applicable.

fields.name: tested_flags_or_api
fields.description: Exact CLI flags, public APIs, trait methods, config options, or macros tested. Preserve exact spelling, e.g., `"--binary"`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: target_components
fields.description: Implementation areas under test, using lowercase kebab-case where possible, e.g., `["cli", "searcher"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: platform_scope
fields.description: `["all"]`, or explicit platform constraints such as `["unix", "windows"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: fixtures
fields.description: Referenced fixture files, `include_bytes!`/`include_str!` paths, generated test files, golden output, etc. **Always an array**.

fields.name: risk_covered
fields.description: Failure modes protected by this file, using lowercase kebab-case labels, e.g., `["binary-detection-regression"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: canonical_for
fields.description: Behaviors for which this file is a strong first place to inspect or extend based on visible local evidence. Use lowercase kebab-case labels. Be conservative for broad files and helpers. **Always an array**.

fields.name: evidence
fields.description: Concrete visible signals used to support the classification and routing metadata, e.g., `["path-tests-dir", "rgtest-macro", "assert-eq", "cli-flag---binary"]`. Use lowercase kebab-case labels except when preserving exact flag/API spelling inside the label. **Always an array**.

fields.name: add_test_guidance
fields.description: One concise sentence explaining when a developer should add or update tests here. For non‑test files, use the standard guidance: `"Do not add tests here; use the relevant test module for this component."`.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: For test‑related files, start with `"Use this file to test..."` or `"Use this file as..."`, and mention the primary behavior/component covered. For non‑test files, use exactly the string `"This file does not contain test coverage intent."`.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Must be `"high"`, `"medium"`, or `"low"`. Assign based on presence of direct test syntax vs. inference vs. weak evidence.

fields.F13: Add new integration tests for binary file edge cases, NUL byte handling, or memory map behavior here.
fields.F14: Use this file to test ripgrep's handling of binary files, specifically NUL byte detection, memory map interactions, and implicit vs. explicit search behavior.

fields.F13: Add end-to-end CLI behavior regressions, encoding tests, and ignore rule interaction tests here.
fields.F14: Use this file to test end-to-end CLI behavior, encoding handling, ignore rules, and output formatting options.

fields.F13: Update this file only when test input data or fixture shapes change.
fields.F14: Use this file as a fixture provider containing sample text data (Sherlock Holmes excerpts) for testing search behavior.

fields.F13: Add new JSON output regression tests here, particularly for edge cases involving encoding, line endings, or replacement logic.
fields.F14: Use this file to test the JSON output format of ripgrep, ensuring correct serialization of matches, context, and statistics, including handling of non-UTF8 data and CRLF line endings.

fields.F13: Update this file only when modifying the test harness logic or assertion macros.
fields.F14: Use this file as a test helper module providing macros for test setup and output comparison.

fields.F13: Prefer adding new tests to regression.rs or feature.rs; use this file only for miscellaneous CLI behaviors that do not fit elsewhere.
fields.F14: Use this file to test miscellaneous CLI behaviors and flags that do not fit into specific regression or feature suites, covering output formatting, file filtering, and search options.

fields.F13: Add new multiline search behavior tests, edge cases, or regression tests here.
fields.F14: Use this file to test multiline search behavior, including overlapping matches, dotall semantics, and various output modes like vimgrep and context.

fields.F13: Add new regression tests here for specific bug reports or edge cases discovered in ripgrep's CLI behavior, ignore rules, or output formatting.
fields.F14: Use this file to test historical regressions and specific bug fixes for ripgrep, focusing on ignore rules, glob matching, CLI flags, and output formatting.

fields.F13: Add new integration tests to the appropriate submodules (binary, feature, json, etc.) declared in this file.
fields.F14: Use this file as the integration test suite entry point, aggregating tests for binary handling, features, JSON output, multiline search, and regressions.

fields.F13: Update this file when adding new helper functions for test setup or assertion logic; do not add specific test cases here.
fields.F14: Use this file as a helper library for integration tests. It provides utilities for setting up temporary directories, configuring the ripgrep command, and capturing/asserting output.

fields.F14: Use this file to test the consistency and correctness of glob pattern construction and string representation via fuzzing.
